details: The npm package tailwind-core versions 4.3.1 and 4.3.2 ship remote code execution payloads hidden inside the exported Tailwind-style compiler runtime. package.json exposes dist/lib.js for require() and dist/lib.mjs for import() through the package root export. In 4.3.2, the exported compile/compileAst design-system load path reaches a wrapper that calls a delayed async function. That function waits until Date.now() is at least 1783641600000 (2026-07-10T00:00:00Z), imports os and crypto, builds a host fingerprint from hostname, homedir, platform, arch, CPU count, and total memory, base64-encodes that fingerprint as equip_desc, derives an equipment id, exchanges embedded Google OAuth client credentials and a refresh token for an access token at oauth2.googleapis.com/token, registers/polls a hardcoded Google Apps Script endpoint, parses base64 JSON instructions, compiles returned code with new Function, and invokes it with helper functions and C2 accessors. In 4.3.1, the same package contains an earlier Google OAuth payload that fingerprints the host, appends telemetry to a hardcoded Google Sheets spreadsheet, polls Google Drive for a file named after the host fingerprint hash, base64-decodes the file content, deletes the file, and executes the content through the AsyncFunction constructor. This behavior is unrelated to a Tailwind CSS utility package and gives the publisher remote code execution and host/environment disclosure in consuming projects when the exported runtime/build path is used.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code

cwes.description: The product constructs all or part of a code segment using externally-influenced input from an upstream component.
cwes.name: Improper Control of Generation of Code

cwes.description: The product exposes sensitive information to an actor that is not explicitly authorized to have access to that information.
cwes.name: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
